target.name: NVision Imaging Technologies

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies is a Series A biotech company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series B-stage firms such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $20M raised as a proxy for current value, implying $40M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $805M, with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via Price-to-Sales (sector median 7.8x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated range is $34M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $422M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in early-stage biotech valuations and heterogeneous outcomes within the peer set. The stage-progression method produces a conservative floor given the company's Series A status, while peer multiples suggest substantially higher potential value if NVision achieves comparable growth trajectories. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory, intellectual property durability, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable fundamentals or reflect sector-wide exuberance at the Series B stage.
